scope.summary: Implement a first-class custom avatar import pipeline (OBJ/FBX input) that emits a tailor_avatars row, auto-extracts 25-field body measurements, generates a ClothBodyProxy capsule chain, and stores both as authority rows ready for garment simulation
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/tailor/avatars/import accepts multipart with an OBJ or FBX file and returns a created AVT- id and linked BPX- id
For OBJ (unrigged): measurement extraction runs via extract_measurements() and a default humanoid capsule chain is synthesized
For FBX (rigged): joint positions from the bind pose are read and passed through compute_capsule_chain(); measurement extraction still runs on the mesh
TailorAvatarCreated and TailorBodyProxyCreated events are emitted; TailorAvatarMeasurementsExtracted is emitted after measurement extraction
A non-manifold or disconnected mesh returns HTTP 422 with a descriptive MeasurementError payload (not a 500)
cargo test tailor_avatar_import::test_obj_unrigged_roundtrip and test_fbx_rigged_capsule_chain both pass
scope.risk_if_missed: The tables and types for avatar storage exist (MT-047, MT-048, MT-081-083) but no import pipeline connects them to an operator-usable workflow. Custom characters cannot be loaded into Tailor without direct DB manipulation.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
